payload.text: I read this as research for a concrete tsgo, Biome, Bun, and browser-bundler setup - I’ll verify current primary documentation and issue reports, then return source-backed configuration guidance.

I read this as completing evidence-backed setup validation - I’ll now validate the proposed config against the current native-preview and Biome CLIs, and extract the official TypeScript 7 compatibility guidance.
I read this as final configuration hardening - the end-to-end scaffold passed, and I’m resolving one Biome 2.5 deprecation so the published config is forward-compatible.

I read this as a research deliverable for a modern TypeScript 7, Biome, Bun, and browser-build setup - I verified the current primary docs and preview issues, then validated the proposed scaffold locally. ## Recommendation Use **Bun for dependency management and scripts**, **Vite for browser transforms/bundling**, **tsgo only for type-checking**, and **Biome 2.x for lint/format**. For a Vite browser application, choose **Vitest** by default because it consumes the Vite configuration and supports Vite-specific transforms/plugins. Choose `bun test` instead for runtime-independent unit tests that do not require Vite behavior. Neither runner uses tsgo to execute tests; keep `tsgo --noEmit` as a separate required check. ## 1. Bootstrap For the native-preview CLI named `tsgo`: ```sh bun add -d \ @typescript/native-preview@7.0.0-dev.20260707.2 \ @biomejs/biome@2.5.4 \ vite@8.1.5 \ vitest@4.1.10 ``` Pin the dev compiler exactly. Preview releases change frequently. TypeScript 7 was published as `@typescript/native-preview` with `tsgo` during preview/beta. The current TypeScript 7 release uses the `typescript` package and `tsc`; switching later is a deliberate migration, not a silent dependency upgrade. ## 2. `package.json` ```json { "name": "browser-app", "private": true, "type": "module", "scripts": { "dev": "vite", "build": "bun run typecheck && vite build", "preview": "vite preview", "typecheck": "tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.json", "typecheck:test": "tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.test.json", "lint": "biome lint .", "format": "biome format --write .", "check": "biome check .", "ci": "biome ci .", "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest" } } ``` When using Vitest through Bun, run `bun run test`, **not** `bun test`: the latter always invokes Bun's built-in runner. ## 3. Strict browser `tsconfig.json` ```json { "compilerOptions": { "target": "ES2022", "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"], "module": "ESNext", "moduleResolution": "bundler", "moduleDetection": "force", "verbatimModuleSyntax": true, "isolatedModules": true, "noEmit": true, "strict": true, "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true, "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true, "noImplicitOverride": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "noUnusedLocals": true, "noUnusedParameters": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "useDefineForClassFields": true, "skipLibCheck": true, "rootDir": "./src", "types": ["vite/client"] }, "include": ["src"] } ``` Why these settings: - `moduleResolution: "bundler"` and `module: "ESNext"` match Vite's ESM-based browser model. They also avoid TypeScript 7's removed `node`/`node10`/`classic` resolution modes. - `isolatedModules` is required by Vite's per-file transform model. It makes tsgo reject constructs which cannot be safely transpiled one file at a time. - `verbatimModuleSyntax` makes type-only imports explicit: use `import type { Foo } from "./foo"`. - `rootDir` and explicit `types` avoid TypeScript 7's changed defaults. TS7 defaults `types` to `[]`, so `vite/client` must be named for asset imports, `import.meta.env`, and HMR types. - `skipLibCheck` does not weaken checking of application code; it avoids third-party declaration noise. Remove it only if validating all dependency declarations is a project requirement. Keep test files out of the app config, then add: ```json { "extends": "./tsconfig.json", "compilerOptions": { "rootDir": "." }, "include": ["src", "test"] } ``` as `tsconfig.test.json`. ## 4. Biome 2.x config `biome.json`: ```json { "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.5.4/schema.json", "files": { "includes": ["src/**", "test/**", "vite.config.*"] }, "formatter": { "enabled": true, "indentStyle": "space", "indentWidth": 2, "lineWidth": 100 }, "linter": { "enabled": true, "rules": { "preset": "recommended" } }, "javascript": { "formatter": { "quoteStyle": "double", "trailingCommas": "all" } } } ``` Biome calls TypeScript, JavaScript, JSX, and TSX the `javascript` language family, so `javascript.formatter` correctly applies to `.ts` and `.tsx`. Use: ```sh bun run format # apply formatting bun run lint # lint only bun run check # lint + format validation locally bun run ci # non-writing CI validation ``` Use the current `rules.preset` syntax. `rules.recommended: true` still works in Biome 2.5 but emits a deprecation notice. ## 5. Vite integration Vite does **not** type-check. It transpiles TypeScript source independently and bundles it; tsgo belongs in the separate `typecheck` script before production builds. No Vite config is necessary for a vanilla browser application. If the emitted JavaScript target must be ES2022, configure Vite separately because Vite does not use `tsconfig.compilerOptions.target` as its build target: ```js // vite.config.mjs import { defineConfig } from "vite"; export default defineConfig({ build: { target: "es2022" } }); ``` A minimal entry point remains conventional: ```html
``` ```ts // src/main.ts const app = document.querySelector("#app"); if (app) { app.textContent = "Hello from TypeScript 7"; } ``` ## 6. `bun test` versus Vitest ### Default for a Vite browser application: Vitest Use Vitest where tests rely on any of the following: - Vite aliases, plugins, asset/CSS imports, or `import.meta.env` - React/Vue/Svelte component transforms - Vite's browser mode or a DOM test environment - A test environment that should closely match Vite development/build behavior Vitest reads `vite.config.*` by default. With Bun as package manager, its command remains: ```sh bun run test ``` not `bun test`. ### Use `bun test` when Vite behavior is unnecessary Use Bun's runner for pure TypeScript units, server-independent utilities, and projects wanting Bun's built-in Jest-compatible API: ```sh bun add -d bun-types ``` ```json { "scripts": { "test": "bun test", "typecheck:test": "tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.test.json" } } ``` Then add `"bun-types"` to the `types` list in `tsconfig.test.json` if tests use Bun globals or `bun:test`. **Important:** both test runners transpile TypeScript themselves. Passing tests do not prove types are correct; retain `tsgo --noEmit` in local checks and CI. ## 7. tsgo / TypeScript 7 gotchas 1. **Preview CLI naming and package migration** - Preview: `@typescript/native-preview`, command `tsgo`. - TS7 RC/stable: `typescript`, command `tsc`. - Pin the native preview exact version; do not rely on semver ranges for a dev compiler. 2. **Compiler API and custom transforms are not ready** - The native project's status table still marks the programmatic API “not ready”. - Avoid tools which import and manipulate the TypeScript compiler API, tsserver plugins, custom transformers, or APIs such as `ts-morph`. - Plain Vite builds are safe because Vite transpiles independently; however, do not expect API-based type-checking plugins to become tsgo integrations automatically. 3. **Do not depend on `tsgo --watch` for the main browser loop** - The project marks watch mode as a prototype without incremental rechecking. - Use Vite HMR for interactive development. Run `tsgo --watch` only as an optional parallel diagnostic process after validating it against the repository. 4. **TS7 configuration defaults and removed options matter** - `strict`, `noUncheckedSideEffectImports`, and stable type ordering are enabled by default. - `types` defaults to `[]`; explicitly list `vite/client`, and test-runtime types where applicable. - `rootDir` now defaults to `./`; set it explicitly. - `moduleResolution: "node"`/`"node10"`/`"classic"`, `baseUrl`, ES5 targeting, and several legacy module targets are unsupported. The proposed Vite config deliberately uses `bundler` and ESM. 5. **Keep a parallel compatibility check during preview adoption** - The native team reports broad TS6 compatibility, but current open issues include correctness problems around large project-reference builds, incremental invalidation, declaration emit, and complex recursive types. - A single Vite browser app with `noEmit` avoids much of this surface. For monorepos, declaration emit, or project references, pin tsgo and periodically compare its diagnostic output against the TS6/TS7 reference compiler. - Reported issue examples: - Complex `three/tsl` types can allocate until OOM: [#4528](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/4528) - Project-reference declaration emit can produce inconsistent diagnostics: [#4262](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/4262) - Incremental build can miss a dependency-driven error: [#4664](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/4664) - Declaration emit with `allowImportingTsExtensions` can emit invalid NodeNext specifiers: [#4616](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go/issues/4616) ## Verification performed In disposable Bun workspaces on macOS arm64, I verified: - `tsgo 7.0.0-dev.20260707.2` accepts and cleanly checks the strict app and test configs. - Biome `2.5.4` accepts the Biome 2.x schema/configuration. - Vite `8.1.5` completes a production browser build after `tsgo --noEmit`. - A minimal test passes under both `bun test` and `bun run test` with Vitest `4.1.10`. ## Sources - [TypeScript 7 native-preview README and status table](https://github.com/microsoft/typescript-go#readme) - [Official TypeScript 7.0 Beta announcement](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-7-0-beta/) - [TypeScript `strict` compiler option](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/#strict) - [TypeScript `moduleResolution` compiler option](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/#moduleResolution) - [Biome configuration reference](https://biomejs.dev/reference/configuration/) - [Vite TypeScript behavior and compiler-option handling](https://vite.dev/guide/features.html#typescript) - [Bun test runner documentation](https://bun.com/docs/test) - [Vitest getting started guide](https://vitest.dev/guide/)
